notes: Internal architectural cleanup discovered in `phase-1-first-workload` PR review and discussed directly with the user, who approved Option C (rename `ProcessDriver` â†’ `ExecDriver`; rename `DriverType::Process` â†’ `DriverType::Exec`; rename `AllocationSpec.image` â†’ `AllocationSpec.command`; add `AllocationSpec.args: Vec<String>`; drop magic image-name dispatch in `ExecDriver::build_command`). The DESIGN wave's authoritative artifacts are `docs/feature/exec-driver-rename/design/wave-decisions.md` and the two ADR amendments at `docs/product/architecture/adr-0026-cgroup-v2-direct-writes.md` (appended `Amendment 2026-04-28 â€” Exec driver rename + AllocationSpec.args`) and `docs/product/architecture/adr-0029-overdrive-worker-crate-extraction.md` (appended the same-titled amendment).

Single-cut greenfield migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: no `#[deprecated]` aliases, no compatibility shim, no feature-flagged old-name path, no `pub use ProcessDriver = ExecDriver` re-export. Both renames land directly. Each step is one cohesive commit. Step 01-01 lands the type-name rename mechanically (`ProcessDriver` â†’ `ExecDriver`; `DriverType::Process` â†’ `DriverType::Exec`); Step 01-02 lands the spec-shape change (`image` â†’ `command`; add `args`; remove magic dispatch) plus the test-fixture argv migration in lockstep.

Two-step decomposition rationale. Step 01-01 is purely mechanical â€” type and variant rename with zero behaviour change; the diff is `git mv`-shaped and reviewer-friendly. Step 01-02 is the substantive change â€” it reshapes the spec, edits every constructor, rewrites `build_command`, and migrates the test fixtures that previously relied on magic dispatch (`image: "/bin/sh"` â†’ inline `command + args`; `image: "/bin/cpuburn"` â†’ inline CPU-busy command). Splitting into two commits keeps each diff scoped to one architectural concern; if step 01-02 needs revision the type-rename commit stays clean.

ADR amendments are unstaged in the working tree at handoff. The crafter MUST commit them alongside the rename PR rather than landing the docs and code in separate PRs. Step 01-02's `files_to_modify` lists both ADR markdown files explicitly; step 01-01 does NOT touch ADR markdown (the type rename is the most mechanical surface and the ADRs already reference both old and new names â€” the cohesive ADR-doc commit lives at step 01-02 alongside the substantive change).

Test directory rename. `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/process_driver/` is renamed to `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/` in step 01-01 (alongside the type rename). The per-file test function names follow (e.g. `process_driver_starts_real_sleep_in_cgroup_scope` â†’ `exec_driver_starts_real_sleep_in_cgroup_scope`). The `mod` declarations in `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration.rs` migrate to match the new directory name.

Acceptance test pinning the rename. Both steps' acceptance comes from existing tests at the renamed path (`tests/integration/exec_driver/start_and_running.rs::exec_driver_starts_real_sleep_in_cgroup_scope`, etc.) â€” these are the post-rename test names. The crafter MUST NOT introduce new tests for this feature; the `phase-1-first-workload` integration suite already covers `Driver::start/stop/status/resize` end-to-end. The rename is a refactor, not a behaviour change, so a new test would test nothing. Step 01-02's quality gates require the Lima sudo integration suite to stay green â€” that is the post-rename acceptance signal.

macOS-vs-Linux invocation hygiene per `.claude/rules/testing.md` Â§ "Running integration tests locally on macOS â€” Lima VM". Step 01-01's quality gates use the default lane (`cargo nextest run --workspace`); step 01-02's quality gates additionally require the integration lane (`cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`) which on macOS routes through `cargo xtask lima run -- ...` per the rule. Mutation runs that include `--features integration-tests` (step 01-02) similarly route through `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants ...` on macOS.

Mutation gate scope. The renamed `ExecDriver::build_command` body is the substantive mutation surface for step 01-02 (the `Command::new(&spec.command).args(&spec.args)` shape replaces the magic-dispatch tree; mutants will try removing `args(...)`, swapping `command` for a literal, etc., and the existing integration tests at `tests/integration/exec_driver/start_and_running.rs` + `stop_with_grace.rs` + `stop_escalates_to_sigkill.rs` jointly kill those mutations). Step 01-01 is a type rename â€” mutation surface is essentially nil (no logic changes); the existing kill-rate gate is satisfied by exiting with no new mutants.

Validation status. The `validation` field at the bottom is `status: "approved"`, `reviewer: "self-architect"` per the user's task brief: "this is internal cleanup, not subject to the standard reviewer dispatch â€” the change shape is mechanical + already discussed with the user." Per `feedback_delegate_to_architect`, ADR amendments and brief.md edits MUST be authored by the architect agent (this DESIGN wave); the crafter does NOT edit the ADRs themselves at implementation time â€” only commits them alongside the code.

Standing user-rule references the crafter must obey: (1) `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations` â€” no shims, no deprecations, no shadow names. (2) `feedback_no_git_stash` â€” focused-subset commits use `git add <paths>` + `git restore --staged`, never `git stash`. (3) `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer` â€” clippy issues that surface during the rename get fixed in-scope. (4) `feedback_no_rename_stubs` â€” no `pub type ProcessDriver = ExecDriver` shim, no `pub use ExecDriver as ProcessDriver`, no leftover `process_driver` module file with a re-export. (5) `feedback_delete_dont_gate` â€” no `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations to silence anything during the rename. (6) `feedback_no_unrequested_ci_lefthook` â€” no CI / lefthook edits unless explicitly requested; this rename does not warrant either. (7) `.claude/rules/testing.md` Â§ "Running tests â€” foreground, always" â€” every test invocation goes through `Bash` with `run_in_background: false` except the post-GREEN mutation pass.

Roadmap-validator length warnings (`STEP_NAME_TOO_LONG`, `CRITERIA_TOO_LONG`, `DESCRIPTION_TOO_LONG`) are non-blocking per CLAUDE.md "Roadmap validator warnings" â€” Overdrive convention deliberately carries scenario-level specificity. Do NOT trim ACs to satisfy these warnings.

reviews.scope: Option C from `phase-1-first-workload` PR review (rename `ProcessDriver` â†’ `ExecDriver`; rename `DriverType::Process` â†’ `DriverType::Exec`; rename `AllocationSpec.image` â†’ `AllocationSpec.command`; add `AllocationSpec.args: Vec<String>`; drop magic image-name dispatch).
reviews.notes: User approved the rename + spec reshape directly during PR review. ADR amendment-in-place pattern (vs new ADR-0030) approved. Two-commit decomposition (type rename, then spec-shape rename) approved. Test directory rename + per-file fn name rename approved. The `cluster_status_under_burst` cgroup-isolation burst test's migration from magic `image: "/bin/cpuburn"` to inline `command: "/bin/sh", args: vec!["-c", "<busy loop>"]` approved. ADR amendments authored by the architect agent (not the crafter) per `feedback_delegate_to_architect`. DISCUSS / DISTILL waves skipped at user direction â€” the roadmap's `criteria` fields carry the AC load.

phases.name: Rename ProcessDriver â†’ ExecDriver and reshape AllocationSpec to honest field names with explicit argv
phases.description: Two-step single-cut greenfield migration. Step 01-01 mechanically renames `ProcessDriver` â†’ `ExecDriver` and `DriverType::Process` â†’ `DriverType::Exec` across the workspace; renames the test directory `tests/integration/process_driver/` to `tests/integration/exec_driver/` and per-file test fn names accordingly; updates module exports + `mod` declarations. Zero behaviour change. Step 01-02 renames `AllocationSpec.image` â†’ `AllocationSpec.command`; adds `AllocationSpec.args: Vec<String>`; rewrites `ExecDriver::build_command` to `Command::new(&spec.command).args(&spec.args)` (the magic image-name dispatch tree is deleted entirely; the setsid pre-exec hook becomes unconditional); migrates every test fixture in the workspace to construct argv inline (the SIGTERM-trap `stop_escalates_to_sigkill.rs` test's `command: "/bin/sh", args: vec!["-c", "trap '' TERM; sleep 60"]`; the cgroup-isolation `cluster_status_under_burst.rs` test's `command: "/bin/sh", args: vec!["-c", "<busy loop script>"]`); commits the ADR-0026 + ADR-0029 amendments authored by the architect agent. Both steps are single cohesive commits.

steps.name: Rename ProcessDriver â†’ ExecDriver and DriverType::Process â†’ DriverType::Exec across the workspace
steps.description: Mechanical type-name rename with zero behaviour change. Five edit classes: (1) Struct rename in `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs` â€” `pub struct ProcessDriver { ... }` becomes `pub struct ExecDriver { ... }`; every `impl ProcessDriver` block, every `impl Driver for ProcessDriver` block, every reference to `Self` inside those impls (left intact by the rename), and every doc comment naming `ProcessDriver` migrate to `ExecDriver`. (2) Enum variant rename in `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs` â€” `DriverType::Process` becomes `DriverType::Exec`; every match arm across the workspace (in `overdrive-core`, `overdrive-control-plane`, `overdrive-worker`, `overdrive-sim`, `overdrive-cli` â€” verify with `git grep -n 'DriverType::Process\|DriverType::Exec'` after the edit) updates accordingly; the impl of `Driver::r#type` on the renamed `ExecDriver` returns `DriverType::Exec`. (3) Module path rename in `crates/overdrive-worker/src/lib.rs` â€” if a `pub use driver::ProcessDriver` (or similar) re-export exists, it becomes `pub use driver::ExecDriver`. The internal module name (`driver.rs`) does NOT change â€” only the type rename. (4) Test directory rename â€” `git mv crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/process_driver/ crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/`. The `mod process_driver` declaration in `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration.rs` becomes `mod exec_driver`. Each per-file test fn renames its `process_driver_*` â†’ `exec_driver_*` (e.g. `process_driver_starts_real_sleep_in_cgroup_scope` â†’ `exec_driver_starts_real_sleep_in_cgroup_scope`); the file-level `// @real-io` doc comment lines update too if they name `ProcessDriver`. (5) Doc-comment migration â€” every rustdoc comment in `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs`, `crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs`, and the integration test bodies that name `ProcessDriver` migrate to `ExecDriver`; `DriverType::Process` references in module-level docs in `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs` migrate to `DriverType::Exec`. The body of `ExecDriver::build_command` is NOT touched in this step â€” the magic image-name dispatch (`if spec.image == "/bin/cpuburn"` etc.) stays as-is until step 01-02. ADR amendment markdown files are NOT touched in this step â€” they migrate alongside step 01-02's substantive edit. Single cohesive commit. Commit message: `refactor(worker): rename ProcessDriver â†’ ExecDriver, DriverType::Process â†’ DriverType::Exec` with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer. Normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
steps.criteria: Workspace-wide type rename complete. Verify with `git grep -n 'ProcessDriver\|DriverType::Process'` returning zero matches across `crates/`, and `git grep -n 'ExecDriver\|DriverType::Exec'` returning the migrated set. The test directory `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/` exists with the same set of files previously under `process_driver/`; per-file test fn names migrated (`exec_driver_starts_real_sleep_in_cgroup_scope`, etc.); `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration.rs`'s `mod` declaration reads `mod exec_driver`. The internal `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs` module name does NOT change (the file still exists at that path). No `pub type ProcessDriver = ExecDriver` shim, no `pub use ExecDriver as ProcessDriver` re-export, no `#[deprecated]` alias â€” single-cut migration. Quality gates: (1) `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` exits 0; (2) `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0 (default lane); on macOS additionally `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` exits 0 (typecheck only â€” runtime is Linux-only); (3) `cargo test --doc --workspace` exits 0 (rustdoc examples in `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs` and `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs` updated to use new names); (4) `cargo xtask dst` exits 0 â€” sim adapters' DriverType match arms have migrated; (5) `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0 â€” no banned-API surface change; (6) `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings` exits 0; (7) `cargo run -p xtask -- workspace-feature-self-test` (or equivalent) exits 0 â€” the workspace convention is unaffected by a type rename. The Lima sudo integration suite stays green: on Linux/Lima, `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests -E 'binary(integration)'` exits 0 (every test that previously ran continues to run; the only changes are file paths and test fn names). No production-code edits to `build_command`'s body or `AllocationSpec`'s shape â€” those land in step 01-02. Single cohesive commit with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; commit body explicitly enumerates the five edit classes above.
acceptance_criteria: Type rename complete: `git grep -n 'ProcessDriver' crates/` returns zero matches; `git grep -n 'DriverType::Process' crates/` returns zero matches.

New names land: `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs` declares `pub struct ExecDriver`; `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs` declares the `Exec` variant on `DriverType`; the impl of `Driver::r#type` on `ExecDriver` returns `DriverType::Exec`.

Test directory renamed: `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/` exists with all files from the prior `process_driver/` directory; the prior directory does not exist (verified by `[ ! -d crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/process_driver ]`). Per-file test fn names migrated.
`mod` declarations migrated: `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration.rs` reads `mod exec_driver` (not `mod process_driver`).
No compatibility shims: `git grep -E 'pub type ProcessDriver|pub use .*ProcessDriver|#\[deprecated.*ProcessDriver|pub type ExecDriver = ProcessDriver' crates/` returns zero matches.

Doc comments updated: rustdoc references to `ProcessDriver` and `DriverType::Process` are migrated to the new names; `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` builds without warnings about broken intra-doc links.

Behaviour unchanged: `build_command`'s body still contains the magic image-name dispatch (the `if spec.image == "/bin/cpuburn"` tree at `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs:147+`); `AllocationSpec` still declares the `image: String` field (no `command`, no `args`); the rename is purely cosmetic at this step.

Workspace gates green: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo nextest run --workspace`, `cargo test --doc --workspace`, `cargo xtask dst`, `cargo xtask dst-lint`, and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings` all exit 0.

Lima sudo integration suite stays green on Linux/Lima: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests -E 'binary(integration)'` exits 0; every test previously passing under `process_driver/` continues to pass under `exec_driver/`.
Single cohesive commit with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
steps.tier: Tier 1 (default lane: nextest unit + acceptance) + Tier 3 (real-kernel integration via Lima sudo on Linux). The integration test rename + mod declaration migration is the load-bearing surface; the Lima suite verifies the rename was complete.

deliverables: Single cohesive commit landing the type-name rename (`ProcessDriver` â†’ `ExecDriver`; `DriverType::Process` â†’ `DriverType::Exec`) across the workspace.
Test directory rename: `tests/integration/process_driver/` â†’ `tests/integration/exec_driver/` with per-file test fn name migration.

No compatibility shims, no deprecation aliases, no shadow re-exports.
steps.implementation_notes: Mechanical type rename. PREPARE: re-read `docs/feature/exec-driver-rename/design/wave-decisions.md` Â§ Decisions D1 + D2; re-read ADR-0029 Â§ Amendment 2026-04-28 (the type-rename narrative). RED skipped â€” this is a refactor with zero behaviour change; the existing test suite is the contract that must continue to pass. GREEN: execute the type rename in this order: (1) `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs` â€” rename `DriverType::Process` to `DriverType::Exec`; verify the `Driver::r#type` doc comment reflects the rename. (2) `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs` â€” rename `pub struct ProcessDriver` to `pub struct ExecDriver`; rename every `impl ProcessDriver` and `impl Driver for ProcessDriver` block; in `Driver::r#type`'s impl body return `DriverType::Exec`; doc-comment migration. (3) `crates/overdrive-worker/src/lib.rs` â€” update re-exports if any. (4) `git mv crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/process_driver crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver`. (5) Update `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration.rs` â€” `mod process_driver` â†’ `mod exec_driver`. (6) Per-file test fn name rename inside the moved directory. (7) `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` should now pass; iterate on every consumer of `DriverType::Process` (use `git grep -n 'DriverType::Process'` if any matches remain). (8) Run `cargo nextest run --workspace` and `cargo test --doc --workspace` to confirm green. (9) On Linux/Lima, run `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests -E 'binary(integration)'` to confirm the integration suite still passes (every test that ran before must run after â€” no new tests, no removed tests, no behaviour changes). COMMIT: single cohesive commit; commit body enumerates the five edit classes; `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer. CRITICAL: do NOT touch `build_command`'s body, do NOT touch `AllocationSpec`'s field list, do NOT touch the ADR markdown â€” those land in step 01-02.

steps.name: Rename AllocationSpec.image â†’ command; add args: Vec<String>; remove magic image-name dispatch in ExecDriver::build_command
steps.criteria: Spec shape complete: `git grep -n 'AllocationSpec' crates/' verifies the field rename took; `git grep -n 'spec\.image\|spec\.command' crates/' shows only `spec.command` (no `spec.image` matches); `git grep -nE '\bimage:\b' crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim.rs` returns zero matches. Magic dispatch removed: `git grep -n 'cpuburn\|/bin/sh' crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs` returns zero matches (the cpuburn branch and the if-image-is-sh branch are deleted entirely; the cpuburn busy-loop script lives in the test fixture, not in production code). The new `build_command` body is one line plus the unconditional setsid pre-exec hook; verify with `wc -l crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs` shows a net line reduction. Test fixtures migrated: every file under `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/*.rs` constructs `AllocationSpec` with both `command` and `args` (verify with `git grep -n 'command:\|args:' crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/`). The cgroup-isolation burst test (`crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/cgroup_isolation/cluster_status_under_burst.rs`) constructs `command: "/bin/sh"` with the busy-loop script as `args` (the same script that previously lived in `build_command`'s cpuburn branch â€” preserve it verbatim). ADR markdown committed in the same commit: `git diff HEAD~1 --stat -- docs/product/architecture/` shows both `adr-0026-cgroup-v2-direct-writes.md` and `adr-0029-overdrive-worker-crate-extraction.md` modified. Quality gates: (1) `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` exits 0; (2) `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; (3) `cargo test --doc --workspace` exits 0 â€” rustdoc examples on the renamed fields pass; (4) `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` exits 0 on macOS (typecheck); (5) `cargo xtask dst` exits 0 â€” sim adapters' Driver impl signatures unchanged; (6) `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0; (7) `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings` exits 0; (8) on Linux/Lima, `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests -E 'binary(integration)'` exits 0 â€” the post-rename integration suite stays green; specifically the SIGTERM-trap test (`stop_escalates_to_sigkill.rs`) and the cgroup-isolation burst test (`cluster_status_under_burst.rs`) continue to exercise the same kernel pressure as pre-rename, just with argv constructed inline rather than via magic dispatch. (9) Mutation gate: on Linux/Lima or via `cargo xtask lima run --` on macOS, `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-worker` reports kill rate â‰Ą80% â€” the new `build_command` body's `Command::new(&spec.command).args(&spec.args)` is the substantive mutation surface; existing integration tests at `tests/integration/exec_driver/start_and_running.rs` (sleep with arg "60") + `stop_with_grace.rs` (sleep with arg "60") + `stop_escalates_to_sigkill.rs` (sh with -c arg) jointly kill mutations that swap `binary`/`args` for literals or remove the `args(...)` call. Run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome. Single-cut migration: `git grep -E '#\\[deprecated|TODO.*image|backwards-compat' crates/` returns zero matches â€” no shim, no shadow field, no feature-flagged old path. Single cohesive commit with `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer; commit body explicitly enumerates the six edit classes + the ADR doc commit; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
acceptance_criteria: Spec shape: `AllocationSpec` declares `pub command: String` and `pub args: Vec<String>` (verify in `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs`); the old `image` field is gone (`git grep -n 'pub image:' crates/overdrive-core/' returns zero matches).

Magic dispatch removed: `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs::build_command` body is `Command::new(&spec.command).args(&spec.args)` plus the unconditional setsid pre-exec hook; the entire `if spec.image == "/bin/cpuburn"` / `/bin/sleep` / `/bin/sh` tree is deleted; `git grep -n 'cpuburn' crates/overdrive-worker/src/' returns zero matches.

Production constructors migrated: `crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs` and `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim.rs` both construct `AllocationSpec { command: ..., args: vec![...] }` rather than `image: ...`.

Test fixtures migrated inline: every `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/*.rs` file constructs `AllocationSpec` with `command` + `args`; the SIGTERM-trap test in `stop_escalates_to_sigkill.rs` carries `args: vec!["-c".to_owned(), "trap '' TERM; sleep 60".to_owned()]`; the cgroup-isolation burst test in `cluster_status_under_burst.rs` carries the busy-loop sh script verbatim as args (the script body is preserved from the pre-rename `build_command::cpuburn` branch).

Sim/acceptance fixtures migrated: `crates/overdrive-sim/tests/acceptance/sim_adapters_deterministic.rs` and `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/acceptance/sim_driver_only_in_default_lane.rs` carry `args: vec![]` (sim driver doesn't exec; args are inert).

ADR doc commit lands in the same commit: `git diff HEAD~1 --stat` shows `docs/product/architecture/adr-0026-cgroup-v2-direct-writes.md` and `docs/product/architecture/adr-0029-overdrive-worker-crate-extraction.md` both modified â€” the architect-agent-authored amendments commit alongside the code (per `feedback_delegate_to_architect`, the ADRs are NOT edited by the crafter, only committed).

Behaviour parity post-rename: the SIGTERM-trap test and the cgroup-isolation burst test continue to exercise the same kernel pressure pre- and post-rename (verified by Lima sudo integration suite passing). The `cluster_status_under_burst` test's `< 100 ms` quality target is preserved.

Workspace gates green: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo nextest run --workspace`, `cargo test --doc --workspace`, `cargo xtask dst`, `cargo xtask dst-lint`, and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings` all exit 0. Lima sudo integration suite passes.

Mutation gate â‰Ą80%: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-worker` (via `cargo xtask lima run --` on macOS) reports kill rate â‰Ą80%. The `build_command` body's `Command::new(&spec.command).args(&spec.args)` is the substantive mutation surface; the existing integration tests jointly kill mutations swapping `command`/`args` for literals or removing `args(...)`. Run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass regardless of outcome.

Single-cut migration: `git grep -E '#\\[deprecated|TODO.*image|TODO.*magic|backwards-compat' crates/` returns zero matches; no `pub use command as image` shadow, no feature-flagged old path, no compatibility shim.
Single cohesive commit with `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer; commit body explicitly enumerates the six edit classes + the ADR doc commit; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
steps.tier: Tier 1 (default lane: nextest unit + acceptance) + Tier 3 (real-kernel integration via Lima sudo) + mutation gate (Tier 1 scope, `--features integration-tests` enabled because the kill-rate signal includes the integration suite).

Default-lane tests green: `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc --workspace` exits 0.
Integration-lane typecheck on macOS: `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` exits 0.

steps.implementation_notes: PREPARE: re-read `docs/feature/exec-driver-rename/design/wave-decisions.md` Â§ Decisions D3 + D4; re-read ADR-0026 Â§ Amendment 2026-04-28 (the cgroup-side narrative) and ADR-0029 Â§ Amendment 2026-04-28 (the type-rename + spec-shape narrative). Examine the current `build_command` body at `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs:128-170`-ish to extract the cpuburn busy-loop script verbatim â€” that script body needs to migrate into the `cluster_status_under_burst.rs` test fixture as the `args` value; do NOT paraphrase or rewrite it. RED skipped â€” this is a refactor; pre-rename + post-rename behaviour is identical for every test fixture, so the existing test suite is the contract. GREEN: execute in this order: (1) Edit `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs` â€” rename `image: String` to `command: String` on `AllocationSpec`; add `args: Vec<String>` field next to it; add rustdoc on both fields explaining the semantics. (2) Run `cargo check --workspace` â€” every consumer of `image` now fails to compile; this is the migration surface. (3) Edit each compile-error site in turn: `crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs` (one site in `JobLifecycle::reconcile`), `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim.rs` (`build_phase1_restart_spec`), every `tests/integration/exec_driver/*.rs` file, the acceptance fixtures. The `stop_escalates_to_sigkill.rs` SIGTERM-trap test gets explicit `args: vec!["-c".to_owned(), "trap '' TERM; sleep 60".to_owned()]`; the `cluster_status_under_burst.rs` cgroup-isolation test gets the cpuburn busy-loop script verbatim as `args` (extract the exact script body from the pre-edit `build_command` body â€” this is the most subtle migration). (4) Edit `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs::build_command` â€” delete the entire `if spec.image == "/bin/cpuburn"` / `/bin/sleep` / `/bin/sh` tree; replace with `let mut cmd = Command::new(&spec.command); cmd.args(&spec.args);`. The conditional setsid pre-exec hook becomes unconditional â€” delete the `if spec.image == "/bin/sh"` guard around it; the `unsafe { cmd.pre_exec(...) }` block applies to every workload. Update the doc comment block above `build_command` to explain the simplified shape. (5) Run `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` again â€” should now pass. (6) Run the workspace gate cascade: `cargo nextest run --workspace`, `cargo test --doc --workspace`, `cargo xtask dst`, `cargo xtask dst-lint`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings`. Iterate on any failures (most likely doc tests on the renamed fields). (7) On Linux/Lima, run the integration suite: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests -E 'binary(integration)'`. Specifically verify `stop_escalates_to_sigkill.rs` (SIGTERM-trap test â€” the inline `args: ["-c", "trap '' TERM; sleep 60"]` exercises the same path as the pre-rename magic dispatch) and `cluster_status_under_burst.rs` (cgroup-isolation `< 100 ms` test â€” the inline busy-loop script exercises the same kernel pressure). Both must pass. (8) Stage the ADR markdown files. The architect agent has already amended `docs/product/architecture/adr-0026-cgroup-v2-direct-writes.md` and `docs/product/architecture/adr-0029-overdrive-worker-crate-extraction.md` in the working tree; the crafter `git add`s them along with the code edits. Do NOT edit the ADR markdown â€” only commit it. (9) Verify the diff with `git diff --stat HEAD` â€” should show the production files, the test files, both ADR files; confirm no incidental edits to other files. COMMIT: single cohesive commit; commit body explicitly enumerates the six edit classes + the ADR doc commit; `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer. POST-GREEN MUTATION RUN: per `.claude/rules/testing.md` Â§ Mutation testing exception, mutation runs use `run_in_background: true`. On macOS prefix with `cargo xtask lima run --` (the `--features integration-tests` flag is mandatory; the kill-rate signal depends on the integration suite). Invoke `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-worker`; wait for completion; parse `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` for the kill rate. Expected mutations to be killed (3 distinct classes): (a) `Command::new(&spec.command)` â†’ `Command::new("")` killed by `start_and_running.rs` (would fail to spawn /bin/sleep); (b) removing `cmd.args(&spec.args)` killed by `start_and_running.rs` + `stop_escalates_to_sigkill.rs` (sleep with no args; sh with no -c arg both behave differently than the test expects); (c) replacing the unconditional setsid pre-exec body with `Ok(())` killed by `stop_escalates_to_sigkill.rs` (the SIGTERM-ignoring sh forks /bin/sleep; without setsid the SIGKILL on the shell does not reach the sleep child and the test times out). Always run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass regardless of outcome â€” leftover mutated source on disk is the failure mode the post-mutation safety step exists to prevent. CRITICAL: do NOT touch the ADR markdown bodies â€” the architect agent owns ADR edits per `feedback_delegate_to_architect`; the crafter only commits them. Do NOT introduce a `pub use command as image` shadow, a `#[deprecated]` alias on the spec field, or any other compatibility shim â€” single-cut greenfield.
